title: NTT DATA Deploys Codex: A 3-Day Failure Analysis by 5 Engineers Reduced to 30 Minutes, 9,000 Employees Are Now Using AI
excerpt: OpenAI released a case study, where NTT DATA Group, a Japanese IT service giant, shortened a complex system fault analysis that originally required five senior
content_html:
OpenAI released a case study, where NTT DATA Group, a Japanese IT service giant, shortened a complex system fault analysis that originally required five senior engineers to spend three days to 30 minutes by deploying the Codex agent. Codex is now widely used by about 9,000 employees at NTT DATA, covering both technical and non-technical positions, becoming a benchmark case for enterprise-level AI agent applications.

From ChatGPT Enterprise to Codex: First Develop Habits, Then Introduce Agents

After NTT DATA formed a global strategic partnership with OpenAI in May 2025, it deployed ChatGPT Enterprise across the company and established an internal OpenAI Excellence Center to promote AI implementation. Internal surveys showed that over 96% of employees were satisfied with ChatGPT Enterprise, and over 95% felt their productivity had improved. This phase helped employees develop daily habits of using AI for research, writing, and content creation, laying the foundation for the introduction of Codex.

Codex's capabilities far exceed traditional programming assistants, as it can independently complete tasks such as investigation, execution, testing, and modification. According to Hiroaki Sato from NTT DATA's AI technology department, Codex changed the company's perception of AI, and the idea that "AI can lead work completion" has had an impact comparable to the arrival of ChatGPT. Shoji Shono, head of the Global AI Office, emphasized that Codex is not only helping developers write code faster, but also opening up a new way of working for every employee.

Expanding from Engineers to Non-Technical Staff, with Security Governance Keeping Pace

Through the "Customer Zero" strategy, NTT DATA treated itself as the first customer for AI applications, encouraging employees to test Codex in their daily work. Non-technical staff have started using Codex to extract transportation costs from credit card statements and fill out travel reimbursement forms, analyze Excel data to generate reports, and batch organize files. Many tasks that previously required engineer support can now be completed independently.

To ensure safe usage, the Excellence Center developed detailed security guidelines, clearly defining the scope of data use, system connection permissions, sandbox mode configuration, automation levels, and manual review requirements. After releasing the usage guide and conducting hands-on training, the weekly active Codex user base increased by 1.4 times. NTT DATA's vision is to amplify the knowledge and professional skills of each employee through its "AI-Driven Enterprise" strategy, and to promote the successful internal experience to customers and society.

content_html_zh:
OpenAI 发布了一个案例，日本 IT 服务巨头 NTT DATA Group 通过部署 Codex Agent，将原本需要 5 名高级工程师花费三天时间的复杂系统故障分析缩短为 30 分钟。 目前Codex已被NTT DATA约9000名员工广泛使用，涵盖技术和非技术岗位，成为企业级AI代理应用的标杆案例。

从ChatGPT Enterprise到Codex：先养成习惯，再引入代理

NTT DATA于2025年5月与OpenAI建立全球战略合作伙伴关系后，在公司范围内部署了ChatGPT Enterprise，并建立了内部OpenAI卓越中心来推动AI实施。 内部调查显示，超过 96% 的员工对 ChatGPT Enterprise 感到满意，超过 95% 的员工认为他们的工作效率得到了提高。 这一阶段帮助员工养成了使用人工智能进行研究、写作和内容创作的日常习惯，为 Codex 的引入奠定了基础。

Codex的功能远超传统的编程助手，可以独立完成排查、执行、测试、修改等任务。 NTT DATA AI技术部门的Hiroaki Sato表示，Codex改变了公司对AI的认知，“AI可以主导工作完成”的理念所产生的影响堪比ChatGPT的到来。 全球人工智能办公室负责人 Shoji Shono 强调，Codex 不仅帮助开发人员更快地编写代码，而且为每位员工开辟了一种新的工作方式。

从工程师扩展到非技术人员，安全治理与时俱进

通过“零客户”战略，NTT DATA将自己视为人工智能应用的第一个客户，鼓励员工在日常工作中测试Codex。 非技术人员已开始使用 Codex 从信用卡对账单中提取交通费用并填写差旅报销表、分析 Excel 数据以生成报告并批量组织文件。 许多以前需要工程师支持的任务现在可以独立完成。

为了确保安全使用，卓越中心制定了详细的安全指南，明确定义了数据使用范围、系统连接权限、沙盒模式配置、自动化级别和人工审核要求。 在发布使用指南并进行实践培训后，Codex 的每周活跃用户数增加了 1.4 倍。 NTT DATA的愿景是通过“人工智能驱动企业”战略扩大每位员工的知识和专业技能，并将成功的内部体验推广给客户和社会。
